description: cleanup_expired_sessions uses the O(n) erase-remove idiom; cleanup_temp_logs and cleanup_debug_marks loop with index and call erase(begin()+i) per match — O(n^2). Unifying onto the naive index-erase pattern re-introduces a 40ms auth-latency stall under session-table load.

findings.rationale: cleanup_expired_sessions calls erase(remove_if(...), end()) — a nested CALL chain with a lambda. The other two iterate index-based and call erase inside a BRANCH inside a LOOP. Completely different control-flow shapes (no LOOP / no BRANCH in the divergent), Jaccard-floor territory.
